chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – The toothbrushing for the Fasting one

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/293)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/293)
text: الصَّائِمِ يَنْزِعُ ضِرْسَهُ قَالَ لَا وَ لَا يُدْمِي فَاهُ وَ لَا يَسْتَاكُ بِعُودٍ رَطْبٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ahmad Bin Al Hassan, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Musaddaq Bin Ssadaqa, from Ammar Bin Musa,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the Fasting-one who gets his tooth extracted. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, nor should he bleed his mouth, nor should he brush with wet stick’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn al-Hassan from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id from Musaddiq ibn Sadaqah from ‘Ammar ibn Musa who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who extracts his tooth when fasting. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, and one must not make his teeth bleed or brush with a fresh twig.’”
